finding: Motor and gearbox are integrated into one unit with electronic shifting.
9 gears, 568% ratio range, about 4000 g, 174 mm Q-factor, 600/800 W peak power, 85 Nm competitive torque.
12 gears, 600% ratio range, about 4100 g, 174 mm Q-factor, 600/800 W peak power, 85 Nm competitive torque.
Closed/sealed housing and 10,000 km oil-change maintenance interval are claimed.
Avinox official production drive units are M2S, M2 and M1; this source does not verify MG Concept specs.
About 2.59 kg; 130 Nm normal max torque; 150 Nm Boost; 250 W rated power; peak power 1500 W only under listed battery/mode conditions.
M2: about 2.65 kg, 110 Nm normal, 125 Nm Boost, 1100 W peak. M1: about 2.52 kg, 105 Nm normal, 120 Nm Boost, 1000 W peak.
Media reports MG Concept combines motor with automatic gearing in a compact housing and targets 2027 launch.
Media reports Gobao X1/X1P eCVT MGUs, X1 at 120 Nm/1200 W and X1P at 150 Nm/1500 W, with February 2027 mass-production timing attributed to BikeBiz.
conditions_or_scope: Media source, not primary BikeBiz check

finding: Official page states motor and transmission are combined into a single unit with automatic continuously variable transmission and optional manual control.
120 Nm, 3.85 kg, 25/45 km/h, 1200 W, 400% max gear range, >120 rpm cadence, Mg housing.
150 Nm, 3.85 kg, 25/45 km/h, 1500 W, 500% max gear range, >120 rpm cadence, Mg housing.
eShift is an electric shifting ecosystem with manual and automatic variants, not a motor-internal gearbox.
Bosch lists 3X3 Electric, Classified Powershift, enviolo AUTOMATiQ, Rohloff E-14, Shimano Di2 families, TRP E.A.S.I. and Gearsensor GS in its eShift partner table.
Maximum possible 120 Nm, 750 W, 600% support, about 2.8 kg; supplied standard values remain 85 Nm and 600 W per footnotes.
AUTO SHIFT uses cadence, torque and speed sensor data, keeps rider in suitable gear and allows manual override.
Pedaling AUTO SHIFT requires Di2 and 10/11-speed LINKGLIDE; coasting AUTO SHIFT works with 12-speed HYPERGLIDE+ or 10/11-speed LINKGLIDE.
Eagle Powertrain integrates e-MTB drive system with Eagle Transmission, Auto Shift, Coast Shift, AXS Bridge Display, AXS Pod and AXS app.
Official page lists Nukeproof, Transition, Propain, Berria and GasGas as Eagle Powertrain bike-brand links.
Automatic system lets riders set a preferred pace and shifts automatically; page emphasizes enclosed low-upkeep transmission and belt compatibility.
Utility hub is listed at 400% range, 300 kg max gross vehicle weight and 120 Nm max torque at 300 kg; Trekking v4 at 380% and 100 Nm at 180 kg.
E-14 synchronizes drive-unit torque reduction and electronic hub shifting; supports single/multi-shift, auto-downshift/start gear and app service functions.
SPEEDHUB benchmark specs include 14 gears, 13.6% increments, 526% range and 130 Nm max input torque/hub.
3X3 claims maintenance-free gear hubs that shift while stopped or under load, with chain/belt and with/without e-drive; system includes NINE/SIX and Electric Shift controls.
P1.18: 18 gears, 636% range, 2700 g. P1.12: 12 gears, 600% range, 2350 g. P-line specs include 250 Nm max torque and 10,000 km service interval.
